canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, MS-COCO (Microsoft Common Objects in Context) is consistently positioned as a large-scale dataset for evaluating a range of vision-language capabilities. The most prevalent use of the dataset is to measure behaviors such as image captioning, image generation (also termed text-to-image synthesis), and cross-modal retrieval. Papers frequently specify using the MS-COCO validation set for these evaluations, often in a zero-shot context. Beyond these primary applications, MS-COCO is also used to assess object detection, segmentation, multimodal understanding, and object hallucination. A smaller set of studies employs the dataset in other ways, such as a source of text prompts for generation tasks, a collection of images for creating adversarial examples, or for evaluating model perplexity on the image-text modality. One definition characterizes it as featuring "complex scenes with multiple objects," aligning with its use across these diverse evaluation scenarios.
